input:
He did not like to quarrel with his people, yet he could not bear to let his nephew down. So he called Muhammad and explained to him what had taken place between him and his visitors. He then said to him: “You see the difficulty of my situation. Do not put my life and your life at risk, and do not burden me with what I cannot bear.” The Prophet felt that his uncle might be contemplating withdrawing his protection so he stated his own position with the greatest emphasis he could muster: “Uncle, should they give me the sun in my right hand and the moon in my left hand in return for abandoning my call, I would not do anything of the sort until God has brought this message to triumph or until I have perished.” The Prophet was under the pressure of such strong emotions that tears sprang to his eyes.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

output.scene_title: The Prophet Stands Firm Against Pressure
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abdul Muttalib ibn Hashim
characters.appearance: Late 50s, darker olive skin, sturdy build, tall. Thick grey-streaked beard with slight reddish henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Kind gentle eyes, slightly stooped shoulders.

output.moral_lesson: Standing firm in your faith, even when faced with pressure and hardship, is essential to achieving your goals and fulfilling your duty to Allah.
output.narrative_text: When the Prophet's uncle, Abdul Muttalib, told him that he might withdraw his protection, the Prophet stood firm and declared that he would not give up his call to Islam, no matter what.

compliance.notes: The scene involves strong emotions but no graphic content. Prophet shown from behind, backlit by doorway light.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Interior of a modest 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing at an open doorway with warm golden afternoon sunlight streaming behind him. Inside, a sturdily built older Arab man with darker olive skin, thick grey-streaked beard with henna tint, off-white thobe and brown cloak, sitting on a woven mat looking concerned, one hand on his chest. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.

metadata.chapter_title: The Call to Islam Goes Public
